true_recommendation: To help relieve the discomfort, try massaging the baby's abdomen gently in a clockwise direction and using a warm compress. Ensure proper feeding techniques to minimize air intake. If symptoms persist or worsen, please consult a pediatrician for further evaluation.

simulation_dialogue.content: Does the baby show any signs of pain or discomfort during the bowel movement, such as straining or distress?
During the bowel movement, the baby's face turns red, which suggests some level of distress or discomfort.

Diagnosis: Possible constipation or discomfort during bowel movements.
Recommendation: Offer more frequent feedings to ensure the baby is getting enough fluids and consider using a gentle baby lubricant around the diaper area to make future bowel movements less painful. If symptoms persist, consult a pediatrician for further evaluation.

self_report: Hello Dr. Li, I gave birth on February 29th this year. Before delivery, I had a serum creatinine test at Peking University Third Hospital, and the result was 48. Two months postpartum, I had a follow-up test at a local hospital, and the serum creatinine was 70.9. The upper limit of the normal range for serum creatinine at my local hospital is 73, while at Peking University Third Hospital, it's much higher at 130. So I’m quite worried—does this mean if I were to retest at Peking University Third Hospital, my serum creatinine would already be over 120? The first result is from the test I took on the day of delivery at Peking University Third Hospital, and the second is from the local hospital two months postpartum. The results from the two hospitals are very different.
true_diagnosis: Anemia

simulation_dialogue.content: When did you deliver, and when did you have your follow-up test at the local hospital? Were there any complications during your pregnancy or delivery that you would like to share?

I delivered on February 29th this year and had my follow-up test at the local hospital two months postpartum. There were no significant complications during my pregnancy or delivery that I would like to share.

Diagnosis: Postpartum renal function fluctuation or possible acute kidney injury.
Recommendation: It would be advisable to retest your serum creatinine at Peking University Third Hospital to get a more accurate comparison with your initial test. Additionally, monitor your fluid intake and ensure adequate hydration. If you experience any symptoms such as swelling, decreased urine output, or flank pain, please contact your healthcare provider promptly.

self_report: I had acute cystitis a while ago, with a bladder wall thickness of about 0.4 cm. The doctor said it was the recovery period and prescribed some Sanjin tablets. Now, how can I tell if the cystitis has healed?
true_diagnosis: You are experiencing pain related to urinary symptoms.
true_recommendation: To aid recovery, I recommend maintaining good hydration, avoiding holding in urine, and practicing proper perineal hygiene. If symptoms persist or worsen, consider follow-up tests such as a urine culture or cystoscopy to rule out other conditions.

simulation_dialogue.content: Are you currently experiencing any symptoms such as frequent urination, urgency, burning sensation during urination, or pain in your lower abdomen?

I am experiencing frequent urination, urgency, and a burning sensation in the genital area after urination, but not during urination. There is also a persistent dry and tight sensation in my anus with a burning feeling.

Diagnosis: Recurrent cystitis or possible other urinary tract infection (UTI) complications.
Recommendation: Given the recurrence of symptoms, it is advisable to consult your healthcare provider for a thorough evaluation. This may include a urine culture and sensitivity test to determine the cause and appropriate treatment. Additionally, ensure you stay well-hydrated, practice good hygiene, and consider over-the-counter pain relief for discomfort. Avoid irritants like caffeine and spicy foods until the issue is resolved.

self_report: My gums are damaged, and I suspect it's due to wisdom teeth. Are wisdom teeth white? I can't eat on the left side anymore. There's also a white tooth on the right side, but it doesn't hurt. Food gets stuck in the gaps. It's been over two weeks since it started. Initially, the right side hurt a lot, and the left side slightly, but then it subsided. These past two days, the left side hurts completely, even making my throat sore. The right side currently has no symptoms.
true_diagnosis: You have swelling and pain in the soft tissues around your teeth, likely due to wisdom teeth inflammation or pericoronitis.
true_recommendation: I strongly recommend going to the hospital for professional cleaning and medication. Localized treatment will be more effective in reducing the inflammation and preventing further complications. If the wisdom teeth are misaligned, consider removing them after the inflammation subsides to avoid recurrent issues.

simulation_dialogue.content: When did you last have your teeth cleaned or visited a dentist? Have you noticed any swelling or changes in the color of your gums around the affected areas?
I last had my teeth cleaned about six months ago. There has been some swelling and a change in the color of my gums around the affected areas on the left side.

Diagnosis: Pericoronitis or periodontal inflammation related to impacted wisdom teeth.
Recommendation: Visit a dentist immediately for an examination and possible treatment, which may include antibiotics if there is an infection, and cleaning to address the gum issues. In the meantime, maintain good oral hygiene and rinse with warm salt water several times a day to help reduce swelling and discomfort. Avoid eating on the painful side until further evaluated.

self_report: My urine is dark red, and the color becomes lighter the more water I drink. These past few days, my legs have been sore from exercise, so I've been lying down for two days. May I ask what's wrong with me, doctors?
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms, the diagnosis is hematuria, possibly due to urinary tract stones.
true_recommendation: I strongly advise you to undergo diagnostic tests as soon as possible to identify the exact cause and receive appropriate medication for treatment.

simulation_dialogue.content: Have you experienced any other symptoms such as pain in your back, fever, or difficulty urinating? Also, how intense was the exercise you did before noticing the muscle soreness?
I haven't noticed any pain in my back, fever, or difficulty urinating. The exercise was quite intense; I did 200 meters of squat jumps and a challenging yoga pose.

Diagnosis: Possible hematuria due to physical strain from intense exercise.
Recommendation: Rest and stay well-hydrated. If the dark urine persists or if you notice worsening symptoms, please consult a healthcare provider for further evaluation.

self_report: Hello doctor, I previously consulted you about lower abdominal pain. Recently, I had a colonoscopy which showed colitis. The doctor prescribed medication, which I took for a week, but the abdominal pain hasn't improved. In fact, it has worsened over the past two days, with pain in the lower left, right, and central abdomen. Should the medication be adjusted in this case?
true_diagnosis: The pain may be related to anxiety-induced somatic symptoms.
true_recommendation: Let's focus on treating the anxiety disorder first and monitor the response. If the abdominal pain persists or worsens, further diagnostic tests such as a CT scan may be necessary.

simulation_dialogue.content: Have you noticed any other symptoms besides the abdominal pain, such as changes in bowel habits, fever, or blood in your stool?

I have noticed some changes in my bowel habits; they seem more inconsistent than before. Sometimes I feel bloated and pass gas frequently, but other times I have constipation. There's no fever, and I haven't seen any blood in my stool.

Diagnosis: Possible chronic colitis with potential complications or an alternative diagnosis such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) or another gastrointestinal condition.
Recommendation: It is advisable to revisit your gastroenterologist for a reassessment. They may consider adjusting the current medication, adding new treatments, or ordering further tests such as stool analysis or imaging to rule out other conditions. Additionally, maintaining a food diary to track dietary patterns and symptoms could be helpful for diagnosis.

self_report: Hello doctor: My last menstrual period started on May 25th and lasted for 5 days. It has been 40 days since my period stopped, and this time it hasn't come. However, a few days before the delay, I experienced mild discomfort similar to menstruation. After an 8-day delay, I bought a test kit and confirmed pregnancy through self-testing. On July 3rd, I went for a blood test at the hospital, which confirmed the pregnancy. I am 28 years old, generally healthy, with no history of contraceptive use or bad habits. This is my first pregnancy. Recently, I've been feeling very tired, sleepy, experiencing breast tenderness, irritability, mild discomfort in the lower body, lower back soreness, and occasional mild pain around the navel, mostly on the left side but sometimes radiating to the right. There is no bleeding or brown discharge. My temperature has been between 36.9°C and 37.2°C in recent days. Is this normal? Could it be an ectopic pregnancy? I'm really scared—it's a mix of joy and worry!
true_diagnosis: You are experiencing nausea, frequent urination, bloating, drowsiness, and fatigue.
true_recommendation: I recommend you undergo an ultrasound to rule out the possibility of an ectopic pregnancy.

simulation_dialogue.content: Have your symptoms, such as fatigue, breast tenderness, or lower back pain, significantly worsened over the past week? Are there any other symptoms you haven't mentioned that you're experiencing?

My fatigue and breast tenderness have remained relatively stable, but the lower back pain has been more persistent. I haven't experienced any other significant symptoms besides occasional mild abdominal pain around the navel.

Diagnosis: Early pregnancy with common pregnancy symptoms
Recommendation: Given the confirmed pregnancy and your current symptoms, it's important to continue monitoring your condition. Schedule an ultrasound appointment to check the location of the pregnancy and ensure it is developing normally. Additionally, maintain regular prenatal care visits to monitor both your health and the baby's development. If you experience severe pain, vaginal bleeding, or any other concerning symptoms, seek medical attention immediately. Continue to rest and manage your fatigue and other symptoms with proper nutrition and hydration.

self_report: What medicine should I take to recover quickly from a cold? Last time I caught a cold with fever due to temperature changes on the subway. This time I'm at home, not sure if it's from catching a chill. Is it related to my constitution? Do I need an antibody shot? (Male, 3 months old)
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms, you're experiencing headaches likely caused by a common cold.
true_recommendation: I recommend taking Compound Daqingye Tablets orally. These will help relieve your symptoms. Also make sure to get plenty of rest and stay hydrated.

simulation_dialogue.content: Can you describe any symptoms your child is currently experiencing, such as coughing, runny nose, fever, or difficulty breathing?
My child is experiencing nasal congestion and feels weak, but there is no coughing, runny nose, fever, or difficulty breathing.

Diagnosis: Mild upper respiratory infection with nasal congestion.
Recommendation: Keep the air humid with a cool-mist humidifier and use saline drops followed by gentle nasal suctioning to relieve congestion. Ensure your child gets plenty of rest and stays hydrated. Consult your pediatrician for further guidance and to check if any over-the-counter medications are suitable for your child's age. Antibody shots are not typically recommended for common colds in infants.

self_report: Hello! My four-year-old child has been frequently having fevers lately, with only occasional coughing and no other cold symptoms. At the end of last year, there were two consecutive bacterial infections with recurrent high fevers, but they eventually resolved. A week ago, the fever returned, and the local clinic diagnosed it as a mycoplasma infection. After intravenous treatment, it improved, but today the fever is back. Why is this happening? The frequency of fevers seems quite high.
true_diagnosis: The child is presenting with recurrent fever, likely due to a mycoplasma infection or other underlying cause given the history of recent infections.
true_recommendation: I recommend continuing fever management with appropriate medication and monitoring. If the fever persists or worsens despite treatment, seek further medical evaluation. For suspected mycoplasma infection, macrolide antibiotics such as azithromycin may be necessary. Ensure the child stays hydrated and is isolated from younger siblings to reduce transmission risk.

simulation_dialogue.content: Has your child's fever been consistent or has it had periods where the temperature was normal? Are there any other symptoms besides the fever and occasional coughing, such as vomiting, diarrhea, or rash?
The fever has not had periods where the temperature was normal; it has been consistently high. Besides the fever and occasional coughing, there are no other symptoms like vomiting, diarrhea, or rash.

Diagnosis: Persistent or relapsing bacterial infection or underlying inflammatory condition.
Recommendation: It is recommended to consult a pediatrician for further evaluation, including blood tests, possibly a chest X-ray, and other relevant investigations to determine the cause of the persistent fever. Additionally, consider consulting a specialist if necessary, such as a pediatric immunologist or rheumatologist, depending on the findings.

self_report: An adult has had a low-grade fever for 7 days. It improves after an injection but worsens again after a few hours.
true_diagnosis: The diagnosis is fever, likely due to a common cold or minor infection.
true_recommendation: I recommend taking the prescribed medication, avoiding cold exposure, and monitoring your symptoms. If the fever persists or worsens after three days, please return for further evaluation, including possible blood tests.

simulation_dialogue.content: Can you describe the temperature fluctuations? Does it rise sharply after the injection, or does it take some time? Also, what kind of injection was given (e.g., antibiotics, antipyretics)?
The fever improves temporarily after the injection but worsens again within a few hours. The type of injection given is unclear as the label is unreadable.

Diagnosis: Possible allergic reaction to an unknown injection or an inappropriate treatment.
Recommendation: Discontinue the current injection and consult a healthcare provider to identify the type of injection and consider alternative treatments. Additionally, monitor for any signs of anaphylaxis and seek immediate medical attention if needed.

self_report: I have had a cold for about four or five days. Currently, I have symptoms like sneezing, runny nose, slightly itchy throat, coughing with phlegm, and sometimes...
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms, it appears you have rhinitis.
true_recommendation: I strongly recommend taking Cefuroxime Axetil tablets as long as you’re not allergic to them. This should help alleviate your symptoms.

simulation_dialogue.content: Do you feel any difficulty breathing or chest tightness along with your cough? Also, have you noticed any changes in the color or amount of phlegm you produce?
I don't have any difficulty breathing or chest tightness. The phlegm is white and hasn't changed much in amount.

Diagnosis: Common cold or mild viral upper respiratory infection
Recommendation: Continue resting, staying hydrated, and using saline nasal sprays to relieve nasal congestion. Over-the-counter medications such as antihistamines for sneezing and runny nose, and cough suppressants if the cough is bothersome, can help manage symptoms. If symptoms persist or worsen, consult a healthcare provider for further evaluation.

self_report: Can I use Chinese meridian moxibustion for vertebral artery stenosis?
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms and test results, I diagnose you with cervical spondylosis.
true_recommendation: I recommend trying the treatment plan we discussed, including the medications and acupuncture therapy. Conservative treatment typically requires at least 20 days to show effects - don't expect immediate improvement within just a day or two.

simulation_dialogue.content: Have you been diagnosed with vertebral artery stenosis? If so, could you provide any medical reports or details about your diagnosis?

I have been diagnosed with cervical spondylosis with a C4-5 protrusion, and my dizziness is attributed to vertebral artery stenosis. I don't have specific medical reports here with me, but I can discuss this further if needed.

Diagnosis: Cervical spondylosis with C4-5 protrusion causing vertebral artery stenosis-related dizziness.
Recommendation: While moxibustion can be used as a complementary therapy for pain relief and general well-being, it may not be the most suitable method for managing vertebral artery stenosis due to potential risks such as exacerbating vascular compromise. It is advisable to consult with a healthcare provider experienced in traditional Chinese medicine and your primary care physician to explore a comprehensive treatment plan that includes medications, physical therapy, and lifestyle modifications. Regular follow-ups are recommended to monitor the condition and adjust the treatment plan as necessary.

self_report: How to treat prostatitis
true_diagnosis: Split urine stream, urination, incomplete voiding, painful urination, urinary urgency, frequent urination
true_recommendation: You should go to a larger public tertiary hospital for a repeat prostate fluid test. It’s best to do a bacterial culture and sensitivity test for the prostate fluid. If Candida is confirmed this time, then consider treatment. So far, you don’t have obvious prostatitis symptoms, and the medications for Candida-related prostatitis have significant side effects. So, if possible, you should get retested.

simulation_dialogue.content: Can you describe your symptoms? For example, do you experience pain, difficulty urinating, or other issues? How long have you been experiencing these symptoms? Have you tried any treatments so far? If yes, how effective were they?

I experience a split urine stream, urination difficulties, incomplete voiding, painful urination, urinary urgency, and frequent urination. These symptoms started recently and have persisted. I haven't tried any specific treatments yet.

Diagnosis: Acute bacterial prostatitis
Recommendation: I recommend starting antibiotics as soon as possible to treat the infection. Commonly prescribed antibiotics include fluoroquinolones or macrolides. Additionally, it's important to stay well-hydrated, take over-the-counter pain relievers like ibuprofen for discomfort, and avoid caffeine and alcohol. Please see a healthcare provider for a proper examination and prescription.

self_report: Yesterday and the day before, I had a fever of 37.8°C and 37.5°C respectively. This morning, I noticed swelling on the left side of my neck, which feels slightly tender to the touch. What could be the cause?
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms, it appears to be an upper respiratory tract infection.
true_recommendation: I recommend you continue monitoring your temperature and schedule a hospital visit for further examination to rule out any underlying conditions.

simulation_dialogue.content: When did you first notice the fever, and did you experience any other symptoms like cough, runny nose, or sore throat?
I first noticed the fever yesterday at around noon, and the day before it was 37.5°C. I also experienced a sore throat over the past two days but no cough, runny nose, or difficulty swallowing.

Diagnosis: Possible lymphadenitis secondary to a viral or bacterial pharyngitis.
Recommendation: I recommend you visit a healthcare provider for a physical examination and possibly some tests such as a throat swab and blood work to determine the exact cause and guide treatment. In the meantime, you can take over-the-counter pain relievers like acetaminophen or ibuprofen to manage discomfort. Stay hydrated and get plenty of rest.

self_report: I sweat easily, especially on my palms and soles, and I'm prone to getting 'heaty'. My penis doesn't stay erect firmly during intercourse, and I ejaculate within four to five minutes. Sometimes it goes soft on its own. How should this be treated?
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms, you appear to be experiencing excessive thirst and dry mouth, which may indicate an imbalance.
true_recommendation: Treatment plans should be tailored individually to avoid misuse or improper application. For a customized treatment recommendation, please consult a doctor directly.

simulation_dialogue.content: Have you noticed any patterns or triggers for your sweating, such as certain foods, stress, or physical activity? Also, how has your sexual function been affecting your daily life and relationships?

My sweating seems to be more pronounced during stressful situations and physical activity. The erectile dysfunction and premature ejaculation have been quite challenging and affect my confidence and relationship.

Diagnosis: Possible combination of hyperhidrosis and erectile dysfunction/precocious ejaculation, potentially linked to stress and anxiety.
Recommendation: It would be beneficial to manage stress through techniques like mindfulness or counseling. Additionally, we may consider testing for thyroid function and prescribing medications such as SSRIs for anxiety if indicated, along with consulting a urologist for further evaluation of erectile dysfunction and premature ejaculation.

self_report: Why do I experience dry coughing and retching every morning upon waking up? This happens for a minute or two daily, and my chest X-ray showed no issues.
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms, you likely have pharyngitis.
true_recommendation: I recommend visiting the hospital for a laryngoscopy to confirm the diagnosis and receive appropriate treatment.

simulation_dialogue.content: Have you noticed any specific foods or drinks that you consume right before bed or early morning that might trigger these symptoms? Also, do you experience heartburn or acid reflux at any time during the day?
I don't notice any specific foods or drinks triggering these symptoms, and I don't usually experience heartburn or acid reflux during the day.

Diagnosis: Postnasal drip or allergic reaction
Recommendation: Consider consulting an allergist for allergy testing, and try over-the-counter antihistamines or saline nasal sprays to see if they alleviate your symptoms. Additionally, managing stress through relaxation techniques or counseling might also help.

self_report: Pain at the top of the spine, neck hurts when moving. When I move my neck, it hurts at the top of the spine. What's going on? (Male, 18 years old)
true_diagnosis: You are experiencing upper limb numbness and radiating pain in the arms.
true_recommendation: I recommend incorporating more physical activity into your daily routine, as this can help alleviate the pain.

simulation_dialogue.content: Could you describe the nature of the pain? Is it sharp, dull, throbbing, or a burning sensation? Does anything make the pain better or worse, such as certain positions or activities?
The pain is a dull ache that worsens with movement, especially when moving my neck. It feels better when I remain still but gets worse when I look down or move my head around.

Diagnosis: Possible cervical muscle strain or minor sprain.
Recommendation: Rest the affected area, avoid movements that exacerbate the pain. Apply ice packs intermittently to reduce inflammation and discomfort. Consider over-the-counter pain relievers like ibuprofen or acetaminophen. If symptoms persist or worsen, consult a healthcare provider for further evaluation.

self_report: The baby is almost four months old and has been eating very little these past few days. She hasn't eaten since last night at 10 PM until this morning, but her energy level is very good. When I try to feed her, she just takes a few sips and then smiles at me. Is this normal?
true_diagnosis: The baby is experiencing fussiness, likely due to digestive discomfort and possible vitamin D deficiency.
true_recommendation: I recommend ensuring the baby gets at least two hours of outdoor activity daily to help with vitamin D absorption and overall well-being.

simulation_dialogue.content: Has the baby had any difficulty swallowing recently or shown signs of discomfort while feeding?
The baby only takes a few sips when attempting to feed and does not show signs of difficulty swallowing.

Diagnosis: Normal variation in feeding patterns or developmental changes
Recommendation: Continue to offer feeds frequently but in smaller amounts. Observe if the baby's interest in feeding improves over the next few days. If concern persists or if other symptoms develop, consult your pediatrician for further evaluation.

self_report: What should I do if I still have a very small phimosis as I'm about to become an adult?
true_diagnosis: Based on your description, you have phimosis.
true_recommendation: I recommend considering circumcision to address the phimosis. Make sure to choose a reputable hospital for the procedure. After surgery, maintain good hygiene and follow post-operative care instructions to ensure proper healing.

simulation_dialogue.content: Can you describe how the phimosis affects your daily life or hygiene routine? Are there any symptoms like pain, discomfort, or difficulty during urination or sexual activity?

The phimosis causes mild pain and difficulty during attempts to retract the foreskin, which can be uncomfortable during sexual activity and hygiene routines. There is no significant pain or discomfort during urination.

Diagnosis: Mild phimosis with associated discomfort during retraction and hygiene.
Recommendation: Consider conservative management such as gentle stretching exercises and maintaining good hygiene. If symptoms persist or worsen, consult a urologist for further evaluation and possible circumcision.

self_report: My nose suddenly became extremely dry—it's either not congested or just very dry. Then, my mouth also became dry to the point where there's a bad taste in it. Especially after washing my face with cleanser, my nose feels particularly dry. Is this psychological, or does my nose have inflammation?
true_diagnosis: Poor sleep quality may be contributing to your symptoms.
true_recommendation: I recommend using a saline nasal spray to moisturize your nasal passages and improve symptoms. If dryness and discomfort persist, or if you suspect internal heat, please consult a doctor for a thorough examination and personalized treatment.

simulation_dialogue.content: Have you noticed this dryness since using a new facial cleanser, or has it been happening for some time? If new, how long have you been using it?
The dryness in my nose started in October and became more pronounced after using a new facial cleanser.

Diagnosis: Irritant contact dermatitis or potential allergic reaction to the new facial cleanser.
Recommendation: Stop using the current facial cleanser and switch to a milder, fragrance-free cleanser. Use a humidifier at home to add moisture to the air. If symptoms persist, consult an allergist for further evaluation and possibly patch testing.

self_report: Throat itchiness, can't help coughing! Found blisters at the base of the tongue (image cannot be displayed due to privacy reasons).
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms, you are experiencing sneezing, nasal congestion, and rhinitis.
true_recommendation: I recommend taking loratadine and Pudilan oral liquid, along with omeprazole to protect your stomach. If you notice yellowish-brown phlegm, it may indicate a bacterial infection, in which case adding levofloxacin would be advisable.

simulation_dialogue.content: When did you first notice these symptoms? Have you had any fever or difficulty swallowing?
I first noticed the symptoms about two to three days ago. There hasn't been any fever, but I do have difficulty suppressing my cough and the blisters at the base of my tongue are uncomfortable.

Diagnosis: Herpetic stomatitis
Recommendation: I recommend taking antiviral medication as prescribed by your healthcare provider. Additionally, maintain good hydration, use over-the-counter pain relievers like acetaminophen or ibuprofen for discomfort, and avoid spicy or acidic foods that may irritate the blisters. Follow up with your doctor for further evaluation and treatment.

self_report: [Question] My two-month-old baby often cries during the day, tends to choke while feeding, and frequently spits up after feeding. At night, the baby hardly cries and feeds well. In the past few days, there have been a few episodes of dry heaving, no fever, and the baby's mental state seems fine. I'd like to ask, how can I tell if the choking has caused aspiration pneumonia? If the dry cough persists or worsens, or if other symptoms like fever or breathing difficulties arise, seek medical attention promptly.

self_report: What's going on with my two-year-old baby having recurrent fever for six days?

true_recommendation: Continue antiviral medication such as Four Seasons Antiviral Oral Solution and oseltamivir. Monitor the fever closely and return for a follow-up blood test and chest X-ray to assess the pneumonia. If symptoms persist or worsen, hospitalization for IV treatment may be necessary.

simulation_dialogue.content: When did the fever start, and has it been continuous or intermittent? Are there any other symptoms like cough, runny nose, vomiting, diarrhea, or rash that you've noticed?
The fever started six days ago and has been intermittent, not continuous. Other symptoms include a cough and phlegm in the lungs. No runny nose, vomiting, diarrhea, or rash have been observed.

Recommendation: It's important to consult a pediatrician for a thorough examination. The doctor may prescribe antibiotics if a bacterial infection is suspected, along with supportive care such as hydration, rest, and possibly over-the-counter remedies for symptom relief. Ensure the baby gets plenty of fluids and monitor for any worsening symptoms or signs of dehydration.

self_report: What's the matter with the thick gray crust on my child's nails?
true_diagnosis: The child is experiencing nail peeling, likely due to temporary nail malnutrition following hand, foot, and mouth disease. The condition is already improving as new nail growth is visible at the base.
true_recommendation: No treatment is necessary at this stage. Simply allow the affected nails to shed naturally while protecting them from further injury. Monitor the new nail growth and maintain good nail hygiene.

simulation_dialogue.content: When did you first notice the crusty appearance on your child's nails, and has it changed in size or color since then?
I first noticed the crusty appearance about a week ago, and it hasn't changed much in size but is slightly darker in color now.

Diagnosis: Fungal infection (likely onychomycosis) based on the duration and color change.
Recommendation: Start topical antifungal treatment, such as clotrimazole or terbinafine, applied twice daily. If symptoms persist or worsen, consult a pediatric dermatologist for further evaluation and possibly oral antifungal medication. Maintain good nail hygiene and keep the area dry.

self_report: Hello, I previously had an abortion. Now I have a lot of vaginal discharge and some abdominal pain.
true_diagnosis: The patient is experiencing increased vaginal discharge, likely due to post-abortion inflammation or infection.
true_recommendation: 1. Complete a full course of anti-inflammatory treatment (5-7 days of injections). 2. Undergo a routine secretion test and culture to identify the causative agent. 3. Strictly avoid intercourse during recovery. 4. Maintain proper hygiene, wear breathable clothing, and avoid cold/spicy foods. 5. If pain persists or worsens, consider hospitalization for intensive care. 6. Follow up with suppository medication (e.g., Kangfu Xiaoyan Suppositories) after acute symptoms subside.

simulation_dialogue.content: Could you describe the color and smell of your vaginal discharge? Have you noticed any bleeding besides the discharge?
The discharge is white and slightly yellowish with no noticeable odor. There has been no additional bleeding besides the usual discharge.

Diagnosis: Possible bacterial vaginosis or mild infection post-abortion
Recommendation: I recommend you visit a healthcare provider for a physical examination and possible testing. They may prescribe antibiotics if an infection is confirmed. Additionally, maintain good hygiene and avoid sexual activity until fully evaluated and treated.

self_report: Irregular menstruation, experiencing some pain during intercourse
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms and description, you have pelvic inflammatory disease and vaginitis.
true_recommendation: For vaginitis, I recommend using Nimixin for a week. For pelvic inflammatory disease, a course of Cravit and metronidazole for two weeks would be appropriate. Please follow up if symptoms persist or worsen.

simulation_dialogue.content: Could you describe the pattern of your irregular menstruation? For example, how often does it occur, and how does it differ from your normal cycle?
My periods have been coming more frequently than usual, about every 2-3 weeks, and they last longer and are heavier than my normal cycle.

Diagnosis: Possible hormonal imbalance or endometriosis, given the frequent and heavy menstrual cycles, along with pain during intercourse.
Recommendation: I recommend you see a gynecologist for a thorough examination, including a pelvic exam, ultrasound, and possibly blood tests to check hormone levels. Avoiding sexual activity until evaluated by a healthcare provider might also be advisable to prevent any potential complications.

self_report: I hear wheezing and snoring sounds when sleeping at night, not sure if it's from the nose or throat, and sometimes there's coughing. What could be the cause?

I first had enteritis at 17 years old (first year of high school), and it recurs every few years. The last episode was a year ago, and a hospital test showed occult blood in my stool, but I didn’t undergo a colonoscopy. The doctor said I was thin and young, so it wasn’t a big issue, advised me to watch my diet, and I recovered after taking medication. Doctor, could this be an intestinal ulcer this time? Even when there’s no pain or sensation, I can still feel the lumps when I lower my head and tuck my chin in on both sides of my jaw!! I can’t help but think about it all the time, but the lumps don’t seem inflamed or irritated. However, pressing on the tendons inside hurts!!! I’d like to ask what’s going on? In the meantime, rest, stay hydrated, and use over-the-counter cough suppressants if necessary. Avoid smoking and exposure to smoke or irritants.

self_report: How accurate is a digital rectal exam for anal fistula? Are there other diagnostic methods?
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms and examination, you have been diagnosed with an anal fistula.
true_recommendation: It is recommended to schedule surgery as soon as possible for a definitive treatment. In the meantime, avoid spicy foods and alcohol, and follow the prescribed medication regimen.

simulation_dialogue.content: Can you describe any symptoms you are experiencing, such as pain, discharge, or discomfort around the anus? This information will help determine the next steps in your diagnosis.
I experience some discomfort around the anus, but no significant pain or discharge.

Diagnosis: Possible early signs of an anal fistula or other anal conditions.
Recommendation: We will perform a digital rectal exam to check for any abnormalities. Additionally, an MRI or ultrasound may be recommended to get a clearer image of the area and confirm the diagnosis. Please come in for an examination so we can proceed with these steps.

self_report: My eyes are bloodshot, not painful or very itchy, but a bit swollen. The doctor said it's eye redness. It got better a while ago but has returned now, seemingly irregularly.
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms, it appears to be related to your cough.
true_recommendation: I recommend using the prescribed eye drops and ointment as directed, maintaining good eye hygiene, and avoiding irritants. If the cough persists, further evaluation may be needed.

simulation_dialogue.content: Have you noticed any patterns or specific times when your eyes become bloodshot and swollen? Are there any activities, substances, or environments that seem to trigger these episodes?

My eyes tend to become bloodshot and swollen in the evenings, and I notice it more when I use the computer for long periods or read in low light. There doesn't seem to be a specific substance or environment that triggers it.

Diagnosis: Digital eye strain or ocular fatigue.
Recommendation: Take frequent breaks while using the computer or reading in low light. Follow the 20-20-20 rule (every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds). Use artificial tears or lubricating drops to keep your eyes moist. Ensure adequate lighting when reading or working at the computer. If symptoms persist, consider consulting an ophthalmologist for further evaluation.

self_report: Dizziness, nausea, chest tightness, shortness of breath, chest pain, difficulty opening eyes (female, 27 years old)
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms, you are experiencing pain, likely related to insufficient blood supply to the heart.
true_recommendation: I recommend getting an electrocardiogram (ECG) done as soon as possible to rule out angina. Please seek further evaluation and systematic treatment at a hospital.

simulation_dialogue.content: How long have you been experiencing these symptoms, and how severe are they? For example, would you say the dizziness is mild, moderate, or severe? And has it been continuous or intermittent?

The dizziness and nausea are moderate, the chest tightness and shortness of breath are severe, and the chest pain is also severe, described as pressure-like and occurring every few minutes. All symptoms have been present for about a month but have worsened in the last few days.

Diagnosis: Acute coronary syndrome (ACS) or angina pectoris, given the severe chest pain and other associated symptoms.
Recommendation: Seek emergency medical care immediately. Do not delay. While traveling to the hospital, try to remain calm and avoid any physical exertion. If you have nitroglycerin, take it as directed by your doctor. Do not drive yourself; consider having someone else drive or call an ambulance.

self_report: Doctor, I feel dizzy, nauseous, and want to vomit. What should I do?
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms, you likely have an upper respiratory infection.
true_recommendation: I recommend drinking plenty of fluids, getting adequate rest, and taking paracetamol if needed for fever. If your condition doesn’t improve, please visit the hospital for further evaluation.

simulation_dialogue.content: When did you first notice these symptoms, and have they been getting worse or better over time? Have you had any recent illnesses or changes in your routine that might be related?
I first noticed these symptoms yesterday morning, and they have been getting progressively worse. I haven't had any recent illnesses but I did travel last week and changed my usual eating schedule.

Diagnosis: Possible motion sickness or viral gastroenteritis.
Recommendation: Rest and stay hydrated. Avoid solid foods until the vomiting subsides, then gradually reintroduce bland foods like crackers or toast. Over-the-counter anti-nausea medications may help, but consult the packaging for appropriate dosing. If symptoms persist or worsen, seek medical evaluation. Consider resting in a quiet, dark room if dizziness is severe.

self_report: I'd like to ask about pain on the side of my child's foot. The pain is mostly on the side. He's been limping when walking for four days now. Recently, the child had a cold with fever and developed bronchitis/pneumonia, and just recovered not long ago. Now this problem has appeared—I'm not sure if it's related to the cold. But even before the cold, he once had pain on the side of his foot, which resolved on its own in a day or two, so this time I thought it would heal by itself and didn't pay attention. When we saw the doctor for the cold recently, he was prescribed vitamin D, and the doctor said there was some rib flaring upon abdominal examination. Images cannot be shown due to privacy concerns.
true_diagnosis: Based on the symptoms and history, your child likely has an upper respiratory tract infection.
true_recommendation: I recommend ensuring adequate rest, monitoring the foot pain, and considering calcium and vitamin D supplementation if needed. If symptoms persist or worsen, please follow up with imaging tests.

simulation_dialogue.content: Has your child experienced any recent falls or injuries that could explain the sudden onset of foot pain? Also, does the pain seem worse at certain times of the day or when performing specific activities?
No, there have been no recent falls or injuries. The pain seems worse in the morning and when walking, but not during other activities.

Diagnosis: Possible plantar fasciitis or stress fracture
Recommendation:

self_report: How can I tell if I have symptoms of hyperthyroidism?
true_diagnosis: The diagnosis includes fever and upper respiratory infection.
true_recommendation: For this 17-year-old female presenting with concerns about hyperthyroidism, I recommend further evaluation with thyroid function tests and blood glucose testing to confirm or rule out the condition.

simulation_dialogue.content: Can you describe any specific symptoms you've been experiencing recently, such as increased heart rate, weight loss, sweating more than usual, or feeling more anxious or irritable?

I feel constantly hungry but never full, have excessive daytime sleepiness after eating, and have noticed fluctuations in my weight without a clear reason. I don't sweat easily and haven't experienced increased heart rate, weight loss, or changes in mood like anxiety or irritability.

Diagnosis: Metabolic syndrome or insulin resistance
Recommendation: It's important to consult with a healthcare provider for further evaluation, including blood tests to check your glucose levels and thyroid function. Additionally, consider adopting a balanced diet and regular physical activity to manage your symptoms.

self_report: Dr. Shang, hello! My father is 59 years old and has been experiencing the following discomforts for over a year. Please help diagnose: 1. Slight pain above the Adam's apple. 2. Excessive saliva, feeling like there's always too much saliva in the mouth, with an inability to spit it all out. 3. A sticky sensation on the upper and lower lips. 4. Slight stinging pain on and under the tip of the tongue. 5. The sensations are more noticeable at midnight and in the morning. Tests at the local tertiary hospital were normal, and local TCM doctors diagnosed it as (yin deficiency with internal heat). However, after taking so much herbal medicine, the symptoms keep recurring, sometimes improving and sometimes returning. I’ve consulted many doctors on Chunyu Doctor with varying opinions, but following their prescriptions hasn’t helped. That’s why I’m consulting you now. Thank you! (Dental exams, laryngoscopy, throat CT, blood tests—all have been done).
true_diagnosis: The diagnosis is a tendency to feel cold, which may be related to yang deficiency leading to floating deficient heat.
true_recommendation: Given the symptoms and examination results, I recommend avoiding excessive cooling or yin-nourishing herbs, as they may worsen the condition. Instead, focus on warming and stabilizing yang. However, an in-person consultation would be ideal for a more accurate assessment and tailored prescription.

simulation_dialogue.content: Could you describe the exact location of the slight pain above the Adam's apple? Is it more to the left, right, or centered? And how does the intensity of the pain change throughout the day?
The slight pain is centered above the Adam's apple, and the intensity seems to be similar throughout the day but is more noticeable at midnight and in the morning.

Diagnosis: Possible thyroid-related issue or neurological disorder affecting salivary glands.
Recommendation: Suggest a thyroid function test (TSH, free T4) and a referral to a neurologist for further evaluation. Additionally, consider a salivary gland function test if the neurological referral does not clarify the cause. Follow up with the results and adjust treatment accordingly.

self_report: Doctor, I had surgery to remove three plantar moles on June 8th, and the stitches were removed on June 22nd. At that time, I noticed swelling in my foot, especially around the first incision. After this period of recovery, the swelling has subsided in other areas, but the first incision and the area extending to the second incision remain swollen. The surface of the incision is red, and there is pain when pressing on it. How should this situation be handled? I’ve undergone three infrared therapy sessions, which have been somewhat effective but slow. Additionally, I’ve been applying warm compresses with a hot water bottle to the wound. I also had a pathological report done, which confirmed that all three moles were junctional nevi.
true_diagnosis: The patient is experiencing pain and swelling around the surgical incisions, particularly the first one, with signs of mild inflammation.
true_recommendation: Continue with microwave therapy as planned. Use topical fusidic acid or mupirocin ointment for the wound. Avoid getting the area wet and minimize walking to reduce irritation. If pain persists, consider taking oral cephalosporin for another week. Monitor the swelling and redness closely, and follow up if symptoms worsen or fail to improve.

simulation_dialogue.content: Have you noticed any discharge from the incision site, such as pus or blood? Also, how severe is the pain when you press on the area?
No discharge has been noticed, and the pain when pressing on the area is moderate.

Diagnosis: Post-surgical inflammation or delayed healing
Recommendation: Schedule a follow-up appointment with your surgeon or a wound care specialist for a thorough evaluation. Continue with the current treatments but consider applying a sterile, moist dressing to the area and using a compression bandage if recommended by a healthcare provider. Avoid excessive weight-bearing on the affected foot until further evaluated.

self_report: I have been coughing for over a month. I don't cough from the time I fall asleep at night until I wake up in the morning, and I don't cough while eating either. However, I cough severely during the day, and my throat feels very dry and itchy, making me cough involuntarily! It's very distressing. I’ve taken a lot of medications, mostly cough syrups, but none of them worked!
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms, you may be experiencing phlegm and coughing, likely due to throat irritation or chronic pharyngitis.
true_recommendation: I recommend further evaluation at the hospital to identify the underlying cause of your cough. Possible tests may include an allergy screening or an examination for acid reflux. Avoiding irritants like smoke and staying hydrated may also help alleviate symptoms.

simulation_dialogue.content: Have you noticed any specific triggers that make your cough worse, such as certain foods, smells, or activities? Also, do you experience any other symptoms like fever, shortness of breath, or chest pain?
I haven't noticed any specific triggers making my cough worse, and I don't have a fever, shortness of breath, or chest pain.

Diagnosis: Chronic irritant-induced cough or allergic cough.
Recommendation: I recommend you see an allergist for allergy testing and consider using a humidifier at home to alleviate throat dryness. Additionally, we may need to explore other treatment options, such as antihistamines or inhaled corticosteroids if allergies are confirmed. Please consult with your healthcare provider for a detailed plan.

self_report: Doctor, hello. May I ask what tests are needed for coughing at the hospital?
true_diagnosis: The symptoms include phlegm and coughing, likely due to a bacterial upper respiratory infection.
true_recommendation: I recommend taking azithromycin, Gan Kang, and compound glycyrrhiza oral solution for 5-7 days. If symptoms persist or worsen, further evaluation with a chest X-ray may be necessary. For night sweats, Astragalus granules can help, but they may resolve on their own after the infection clears.

simulation_dialogue.content: Could you describe the nature of your cough? Is it dry, productive (with phlegm), or does it worsen at certain times of the day or night?
My cough is productive, bringing up yellow phlegm, and it tends to worsen at night.

Diagnosis: Possible bacterial bronchitis or early signs of pneumonia.
Recommendation: I recommend you see a healthcare provider for a thorough evaluation, including a physical examination and possibly chest X-ray and sputum culture. They may prescribe antibiotics if a bacterial infection is confirmed. Additionally, make sure to stay hydrated and get plenty of rest.

self_report: A 1-year-old baby vomits a large amount of milk whenever he coughs. It started last night. The cough is not severe, but the vomiting is. There's no sign of nausea or anything like that. He never vomited milk before, even when he had a cold or throat inflammation (male, 11 months).
true_diagnosis: Based on the symptoms, the baby may have a fever.
true_recommendation: I recommend avoiding antiemetic medication for now and monitoring the baby’s condition closely. If vomiting persists or worsens, further evaluation may be needed.

simulation_dialogue.content: How often does this vomiting occur? Is it immediately after coughing or at other times as well?
The vomiting occurs immediately after coughing and has not happened at other times.

Diagnosis: Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD) or transient esophageal spasm triggered by coughing.
Recommendation: Monitor the situation closely. If the vomiting continues or worsens, or if there are signs of dehydration (such as dry mouth, fewer wet diapers), consult your pediatrician immediately. In the meantime, try to keep the baby upright for at least 30 minutes after feeding to reduce the likelihood of reflux.

self_report: My menstrual period comes three days early every month. The duration is long, lasting about 8-10 days. Additionally, there is blood in the vaginal discharge, often with streaks of blood or brownish color. I also experience dull, lingering pain in the lower abdomen.
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms, you appear to have abnormal vaginal discharge and abdominal pain, likely related to inflammation and the intrauterine device.
true_recommendation: I recommend removing the IUD after a proper examination, followed by anti-inflammatory treatment. Additionally, schedule regular cervical screenings, especially if you’ve had a positive HPV result in the past. Avoid sexual activity before the IUD removal and follow up with any further tests as needed.

simulation_dialogue.content: When did you last undergo a gynecological examination or pelvic ultrasound? Have you noticed any changes in your weight, stress levels, or dietary habits recently?
I last had a gynecological examination about a year ago, and a pelvic ultrasound was part of that check-up. No significant changes in weight, stress levels, or dietary habits recently.

Diagnosis: Possible hormonal imbalance or uterine fibroids
Recommendation: It is recommended to schedule a follow-up gynecological examination to assess current hormone levels and possibly perform another pelvic ultrasound. Additionally, managing stress and maintaining a balanced diet might help. If necessary, your doctor may suggest hormonal therapy or other treatments based on the findings.

self_report: Hello! I suddenly developed a 5 cm long hard lump on the inner side of my left thigh near the connection to the testicle! It hurts when touched! What could be the reason?
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms, this appears to be pain caused by a soft tissue infection and inflammation.
true_recommendation: I recommend going to the hospital to get a blood test to check your white blood cell count. If the levels are high, intravenous anti-inflammatory treatment may be necessary.

simulation_dialogue.content: When did you first notice this lump, and has it changed in size since then?
I first noticed the lump about two days ago, and it has remained at approximately 5 cm in length since then.

Diagnosis: Possible testicular tumor or inguinal hernia
Recommendation: You should seek immediate medical attention for a physical examination and possibly imaging studies such as an ultrasound. This will help determine the exact cause and guide appropriate treatment.

self_report: Doctor, hello. My child has been experiencing frequent nosebleeds for about two months. At first, we thought it was normal because the house was hot and dry, and the child sometimes scratched their nose due to itching. The bleeding isn't consistent—sometimes once a day, sometimes twice, but not every day. It happens occasionally and can be stopped. However, yesterday, out of concern, we took the child for a blood test and coagulation test. Two items in the coagulation test were abnormal, and the community hospital doctor said there were some irregularities. So, I’d like to ask for your advice on this. Thank you!
true_diagnosis: The diagnosis is nosebleeds and rhinitis.
true_recommendation: I recommend consulting an ENT specialist for further evaluation and possible treatment for rhinitis. In the meantime, try to keep the child’s nasal passages moist and avoid nose-scratching to prevent further irritation.

simulation_dialogue.content: Could you please share the specific results of the coagulation tests that showed abnormalities? Also, are there any other symptoms your child is experiencing besides the nosebleeds, such as easy bruising, prolonged bleeding after minor injuries, or any signs of fatigue?
Sorry, I cannot answer your question.

Diagnosis: Possible coagulation disorder or environmental factors contributing to nosebleeds.
Recommendation: Given the recent coagulation test abnormalities and the history of frequent nosebleeds, it is important to consult a hematologist for a thorough evaluation. Additionally, consider using a humidifier in the home to maintain adequate moisture levels, which may help reduce nosebleeds. Ensure the child avoids picking or scratching their nose to prevent irritation. Follow up with the community hospital for further assessment and possibly additional tests to confirm the cause of the coagulation abnormalities.

self_report: I got married just over ten days ago, and recently I've been experiencing persistent itching down there with yellow, clumpy discharge. What's going on? This has never happened before!
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms, you likely have vaginitis, possibly a yeast infection given the characteristic discharge.
true_recommendation: I recommend visiting the hospital as soon as possible to have your discharge tested. Once we confirm the specific type of vaginitis, we can provide targeted treatment. Both you and your partner may need medication to prevent recurrence. Until then, avoid scratching, wear cotton underwear, and maintain good hygiene practices.

simulation_dialogue.content: Have you noticed any changes in your sexual activity or hygiene practices recently that might be relevant?
No changes in sexual activity or hygiene practices recently.

Diagnosis: Possible trichomoniasis or other STI
Recommendation: It's important to see a healthcare provider for a proper examination and testing. They may prescribe antibiotics if a specific infection is confirmed. Additionally, inform your partner to get checked and treated to avoid re-infection.

self_report: Why do I have migraines, dizziness, and pain when drinking, eating, or swallowing after throat tumor surgery?
true_diagnosis: You are experiencing headaches, which are likely due to post-operative inflammation and healing.
true_recommendation: I recommend continuing pain management as needed, staying hydrated, and following up with your surgeon for personalized guidance. Recovery takes time, so be patient and allow your body to heal.

simulation_dialogue.content: When did you undergo your throat tumor surgery, and how long ago was it? Have you noticed any changes in your symptoms over time?
I underwent my throat tumor surgery two months ago, and my symptoms started two days ago, which is a change from the initial recovery period where I felt better.

Diagnosis: Possible post-surgical complications such as infection or inflammation, with migraines and dizziness possibly indicating other underlying conditions.
Recommendation: Please contact your surgeon or healthcare provider immediately for a follow-up evaluation. They may need to perform further tests to determine the cause of your symptoms and provide appropriate treatment. In the meantime, stay hydrated and monitor your symptoms closely.

self_report: Itching and redness in the vulva. Can I use Metronidazole Clotrimazole Vaginal Gel? No contraceptive measures were taken, and I'm currently not pregnant.
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms, you appear to have abnormal vaginal discharge, possibly due to an infection.
true_recommendation: I recommend using a broad-spectrum antifungal like Metronidazole Clotrimazole Vaginal Gel as a first-line treatment. However, for a definitive diagnosis, you should undergo a vaginal discharge test. Avoid sexual intercourse during treatment and maintain good hygiene by changing underwear frequently.

simulation_dialogue.content: When did these symptoms start, and have you noticed anything that seems to trigger them, such as new products used in the genital area or changes in hygiene practices?
These symptoms started after my last menstrual period, and they seem to be triggered by changes in hygiene practices and possibly new products used in the genital area.

Diagnosis: Possible irritant contact dermatitis or yeast infection
Recommendation: I recommend visiting a healthcare provider for a proper examination and possibly a vaginal swab test to determine the exact cause of your symptoms. Based on the results, your healthcare provider will recommend the appropriate treatment. In the meantime, avoid using any new products in the genital area and maintain gentle hygiene practices.

self_report: Doctor, my last year's physical examination showed emphysema, and this year it's bilateral emphysema with fibrosis in the right lung. Is it serious?
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms and examination results, you have a chronic cough associated with emphysema and pulmonary fibrosis.
true_recommendation: I recommend managing your symptoms with cough suppressants and expectorants as needed. It's also important to avoid smoking and strenuous activities to prevent further lung damage. Regular follow-ups will help monitor the progression of your condition.

simulation_dialogue.content: Have you noticed any new symptoms or changes in your breathing since your last physical examination?
I've been experiencing more frequent episodes of sudden choking coughs, especially when I wake up, and I feel more fatigued than before, even when climbing small slopes.

Diagnosis: Possible exacerbation of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) with emphysema and fibrosis.
Recommendation: It is important to schedule an appointment with a pulmonologist for a thorough evaluation, which may include a chest X-ray or CT scan, pulmonary function tests, and possibly a blood test. In the meantime, continue using your prescribed medications as directed and avoid known triggers such as smoke and pollution. If your symptoms worsen, seek medical attention immediately.

self_report: What should I do if my baby always chokes when drinking water?
true_diagnosis: The baby is experiencing sneezing, nasal congestion, runny nose, and coughing.
true_recommendation: I recommend letting the baby take breaks during feeding - feed for a while, then pause to rest.

simulation_dialogue.content: How often does your baby choke when drinking water, and is it happening with every attempt or just occasionally? Also, could you describe how the choking looks like during these instances?
The baby occasionally chokes, about a few times per feeding, but does not turn purple during these episodes.

Diagnosis: Occasional choking during feeding, possibly due to air swallowing or improper posture.
Recommendation: Ensure the baby is sitting upright during feedings and take breaks between sips to allow time for swallowing. Consider using a slower flow nipple or a specialized bottle designed to reduce air intake. If the problem persists or worsens, consult a pediatrician for further evaluation.

self_report: Hello, my Helicobacter pylori test result was 1000. I took the quadruple therapy prescribed by the doctor for two weeks and have been off the medication for a week now. I've had diarrhea these past two days, with blood appearing twice.
true_diagnosis: The symptoms include black stool, bleeding, and diarrhea.
true_recommendation: I recommend getting all the tests done. It’ll give you peace of mind if everything is fine.

simulation_dialogue.content: Have you noticed any other symptoms besides diarrhea and blood in your stools? For example, abdominal pain, fever, or vomiting?
I have not experienced any abdominal pain, fever, or vomiting.

Diagnosis: Possible residual bleeding from a peptic ulcer or side effect of quadruple therapy.
Recommendation: Please seek medical attention immediately for further evaluation and possible endoscopy. Continue monitoring your symptoms and report any changes promptly.

self_report: I have a severe and prolonged cough every time I catch a cold.
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms, you have an upper respiratory tract infection accompanied by coughing.
true_recommendation: To help with your current condition, I recommend taking prescribed antibiotics if not allergic, along with cough suppressants. For long-term improvement, focus on maintaining good sleep habits and regular exercise to strengthen your immune system.

simulation_dialogue.content: How long does your cough typically last after you catch a cold, and does it affect your ability to perform daily activities?
My cough lasts about seven or eight days every time I catch a cold, and it does produce a lot of phlegm, which can make it hard to do some daily activities.

Diagnosis: Chronic post-nasal drip or chronic bronchitis
Recommendation: Consider seeing an ENT specialist or pulmonologist for further evaluation. In the meantime, try using a humidifier, drinking plenty of fluids, and consider over-the-counter expectorants to help manage the cough and phlegm.

self_report: A one-year-old baby suddenly vomited and then had diarrhea
true_diagnosis: Based on the symptoms described, this appears to be a case of diarrhea, possibly caused by dietary factors or a viral infection.
true_recommendation: Continue administering oral rehydration solutions to prevent dehydration. Monitor their fluid intake closely and ensure they rest. If symptoms persist or worsen, seek further medical evaluation to rule out infections or other underlying causes.

simulation_dialogue.content: When did the vomiting and diarrhea start, and has it been continuous or intermittent?
The vomiting started suddenly, followed by diarrhea that has been continuous for three days.

Diagnosis: Gastroenteritis or possible food intolerance
Recommendation: Keep the baby hydrated with oral rehydration solutions. Monitor for signs of dehydration such as dry mouth, no tears when crying, and fewer wet diapers. Consult a pediatrician for further evaluation and medication if necessary.

self_report: I had my wisdom tooth extracted at 3 pm this afternoon. Now my neck is swollen, I have a headache, and swallowing is very painful. Images cannot be displayed due to privacy concerns.
true_diagnosis: You are experiencing neck pain, which is likely a postoperative reaction after the wisdom tooth extraction.
true_recommendation: Continue taking the prescribed medications, including pain relievers like ibuprofen if needed. Avoid sucking the wound or disturbing it unnecessarily. Refrain from eating or drinking for the next two hours, and wait until tomorrow to brush your teeth or rinse your mouth. If the pain or swelling does not improve in three days, please return for further evaluation.

simulation_dialogue.content: Can you describe the intensity and location of the pain? Is it constant or does it come and go? And how swollen does your neck feel compared to the rest of your body?

The neck pain is moderate to severe and located on the right side, it's constant but can be more noticeable when turning my head. The neck feels slightly more swollen compared to the other parts of my body.

Diagnosis: Possible infection or inflammation post-wisdom tooth extraction.
Recommendation: Please contact your dentist or oral surgeon as soon as possible for an evaluation. They may prescribe antibiotics and recommend pain management options. In the meantime, try to stay hydrated and avoid strenuous activities.

self_report: Pregnant woman, having diarrhea, stomach still gurgling, received an IV last night for dehydration prevention and was given some anti-diarrheal medication, but still experiencing diarrhea. What's going on?
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms, you are experiencing diarrhea.
true_recommendation: I recommend maintaining a light diet, drinking plenty of water, keeping your abdomen warm, and avoiding medication as much as possible since you are pregnant.

simulation_dialogue.content: How long has the diarrhea been ongoing, and has it been consistent or intermittent? Are there any other symptoms such as fever, vomiting, or abdominal pain?
The diarrhea started a few days ago and has been somewhat intermittent. There is no fever, but there is mild abdominal pain and occasional nausea.

Diagnosis: Possible gastroenteritis or food intolerance
Recommendation: Continue monitoring the symptoms. Ensure adequate hydration, and consider consulting a specialist if the symptoms persist or worsen. A dietitian may also be able to provide guidance on dietary adjustments to alleviate symptoms. Regular prenatal check-ups are crucial during this time.

self_report: Have you had a dry cough for about 3 weeks that hasn't gone away? It doesn't happen at night but occurs during the day, and your throat feels itchy.
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms, you have a dry cough, likely due to heightened airway reactivity during the recovery phase from a previous illness.
true_recommendation: I recommend getting plenty of rest, avoiding strenuous exercise, and monitoring your symptoms for a while. If the cough persists or worsens, consider further evaluation.

simulation_dialogue.content: Have you noticed any other symptoms besides the dry cough and itchy throat, such as fever, shortness of breath, or body aches?
No, I haven't noticed any fever, shortness of breath, or body aches.

Diagnosis: Persistent dry cough and itchy throat could be indicative of allergic rhinitis or irritation from environmental factors.
Recommendation: I recommend avoiding potential irritants like dust, smoke, or allergens. Consider using over-the-counter antihistamines and saline nasal sprays. If symptoms persist, please consult an allergist for further evaluation and possible allergy testing.

self_report: A week ago, the little finger on my left hand felt a bit numb but there was no pain or itching, and there were no visible changes. It didn't affect daily life or function, and the tactile sensation was normal. This morning, I noticed the ring finger also started feeling slightly numb. I don’t know the cause or how to treat it.
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms, you may be experiencing limb weakness and headaches, which could be related to nerve irritation or cervical spine issues.
true_recommendation: To alleviate your symptoms, I recommend reducing prolonged phone usage, avoiding keeping your head lowered for extended periods, and performing neck exercises. You can also try gentle massage or applying heat to the neck to improve blood circulation. If the numbness persists or worsens, or if you develop additional symptoms like chest tightness, further evaluation may be necessary.

simulation_dialogue.content: Have you noticed any swelling, discoloration, or changes in skin temperature around the affected fingers? Also, do you engage in any activities that might put pressure on your hands, such as typing or playing musical instruments?
No, there is no swelling, discoloration, or change in skin temperature. I do use my phone frequently, which involves holding my hand in one position for extended periods.

Diagnosis: Possible carpal tunnel syndrome or nerve compression due to repetitive use.
Recommendation: I recommend you see a healthcare provider for a thorough examination. They may perform nerve conduction studies or other tests to confirm the diagnosis and recommend appropriate treatments, which could include rest, wrist splints, anti-inflammatory medications, or physical therapy.

self_report: Can omeprazole, mosapride, and zhengchangsheng treat gastritis and enteritis?
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms, the diagnosis is enteritis.
true_recommendation: I recommend adding mosapride to your treatment.

simulation_dialogue.content: Are you currently experiencing any symptoms that might suggest gastritis or enteritis, such as stomach pain, nausea, or changes in bowel habits?

Yes, I experience stomach discomfort, frequent flatulence, belching, and hiccups, along with a bad taste in my mouth similar to stool, and changes in bowel habits like soft, sticky stools with undigested food.

Diagnosis: Dyspepsia and possible Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS) based on the symptoms of stomach discomfort, flatulence, belching, hiccups, and changes in bowel habits.
Recommendation: Omeprazole can help reduce stomach acid, which might alleviate some of your symptoms. Mosapride can aid in improving gut motility, which may help with bloating and gas. Zhengchangsheng is a traditional Chinese medicine that can also help regulate digestive functions. It's important to follow the dosing instructions provided by your pharmacist or doctor. Additionally, consider dietary modifications such as avoiding spicy, fatty foods, and increasing fiber intake gradually. Regular exercise and stress management techniques may also be beneficial. If symptoms persist or worsen, please consult a healthcare professional for further evaluation.

self_report: Sometimes my stomach hurts really badly, and when it does, I have to go to the bathroom with diarrhea. I also feel nauseous and vomit.
true_diagnosis: Based on your symptoms, you have acute gastroenteritis.
true_recommendation: I recommend getting a blood test for a complete blood count and a stool test for routine analysis plus occult blood to confirm the diagnosis and guide treatment.

simulation_dialogue.content: Have you recently consumed any new foods or beverages that might have caused an allergic reaction or upset your stomach? Also, describe the nature of your abdominal pain—does it start suddenly or gradually, and does it get better or worse with certain activities?

self_report: Mother, 56 years old, currently experiencing persistent coughing with sticky phlegm in the throat that is difficult to expel. She has already taken medications, including pneumonia granules, cefixime tablets, Nin Jiom Pei Pa Koa, and oxybutynin tablets. The doctor suggested getting a chest X-ray at the hospital but also advised waiting for some time before going, as we are currently discouraged from visiting our local hospital. Initially, around the 24th of the lunar new year, she caught a cold from working early at 4-5 AM during very cold days, which led to coughing. She then stopped working and received IV fluids for three days. After recovering from the cold, around the 3rd day of the lunar new year, I followed the doctor’s advice not to go to the hospital and instead bought her lung-clearing granules and cefixime tablets. After taking them, her condition improved significantly, with almost no coughing except in the early morning, and her throat no longer felt itchy. Thinking she was cured, I consulted the doctor again and bought oxybutynin tablets, Nin Jiom Pei Pa Koa, and cefixime tablets for her to take together. Unexpectedly, her throat is now itchy again, and she has sticky, white phlegm that requires great effort to cough up. Since the 27th of the lunar new year, she hadn’t gone out until this afternoon when she wore a mask and went out for about two hours before returning.

Ensure adequate hydration and monitor the symptoms. If there’s no improvement within two days, further evaluation at the hospital may be necessary, provided it’s safe to visit.
